message:
fix(test): repair API drift across 6 test files (ft-cke6c)

Six unrelated production-API drifts had silently rotted six test
files, blocking `cargo test -p frankenterm-core` (and therefore
`cargo test toon`) from compiling. Diagnosed and fixed each:

## 1. SearchOptions.include_highlights — added (ft-okhhj)
- tests/e2e_capture_search.rs:163 missed the `include_highlights`
  field in a struct literal. Other 3 test files using SearchOptions
  use `..SearchOptions::default()` so they auto-pick-up the new
  field.

## 2. IndexLookup trait — combined to lookup_event_offset
- The trait used to expose split has_event_id (existence) +
  get_log_offset (offset). Now it's one method:
  `lookup_event_offset(...) -> Result<Option<u64>, _>` where
  Ok(None) = missing and Ok(Some(offset)) = present. Three test
  files implemented the OLD trait surface:
  * tests/recorder_recovery_drills.rs:214 (DrillLookup)
  * tests/tantivy_integration_tests.rs:224 (DocLookup)
  * tests/tantivy_reindex_labruntime.rs:231 (MockIndexLookup)
  All three now implement only `lookup_event_offset` + `count_total`.

## 3. IntegrityCheckConfig.batch_size — added
- New required `batch_size: usize` field. 13 struct literals across
  two test files (tantivy_integration_tests.rs + tantivy_reindex_-
  labruntime.rs) all picked up `batch_size: 1000` (matching the
  Default impl's value).

## 4. CautService / CautError — moved to ext-trait pattern
- `provider_hint()`, `from_provider()`, and `remediation()` are now
  methods on the `CautServiceProviderExt` and
  `CautErrorRemediationExt` traits (caut.rs:33, 66). tests/proptest_-
  caut.rs's import block now brings those traits into scope.

## 5. ScanPipeline returns TriggerCategoryCounts (not HashMap)
- The pipeline's per-category trigger counts moved from
  `HashMap<TriggerCategory, u64>` to a packed `[u64; N]` struct
  `TriggerCategoryCounts`, gaining O(1) lookup + zero allocation.
  tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs:
  - chunked_trigger_counts() helper return type follows suit
  - the `for (cat, count) in &base_result.counts` loop becomes
    `base_result.counts.iter()` (the new struct exposes .iter()
    yielding owned (TriggerCategory, u64) pairs in
    TriggerCategory::all order)
  - prop_assert_eq! comparisons now compare TriggerCategoryCounts
    directly (PartialEq + Eq derived per the new struct)

## Verified
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --tests → exit 0
  (1m42s full check; full lib + every test target compiles)
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test toon_golden toon → 5 passed
  (the focused TOON suite the bead called out as gated)

The broader `cargo test toon` filtered run was blocked at compile-
time by these drifts; with the fixes landed, every test binary that
filter touches now compiles cleanly. Long-running campaigns of the
full test suite are the deployment step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

                     highlight_prefix: Some("[[".to_string()),
                     highlight_suffix: Some("]]".to_string()),
